title: Angular Material crashes
author: dayanfcosta

comments.author: dayanfcosta
comments.body:
The described situation is happening in Chrome browser, when I tested on FireFox ones, my page doesn't even loaded and the browser show too many times the message `A web page is slowing down your browser` 

EDIT:
my imports on top of .ts file are:
```
import { Workbook, Worksheet } from 'exceljs';
import * as fileSaver from 'file-saver';
```

comments.author: adesheddie1
comments.body: Facing same issue... My browser just hangs up. Did you find any solution ?

comments.author: dayanfcosta
comments.body: @adesheddie1 not yet, I removed this lib from my project and started to generate my excel files on backend side.

comments.author: myin142
comments.body:
I have a similar issue. When I want to navigate within the same route module then nothing happens, only the URL changes. When I click it the second time, the whole page reloads.
I import it exactly like you.

comments.author: dayanfcosta
comments.body: @myin142 the behavior is exactly as you described, happen with routes and any other material's component. I just stopped to use the library until I have time to find out the real bug and a solution for this.

comments.author: myin142
comments.body:
Inside lib/exceljs.browser.js is a line:
```javascript
require('core-js/modules/es.promise');
```
Inside this module is something that causes this problem. I do not really have the time do pinpoint the location. But it does not seem to be a problem in this library. And the code still works if I remove it, so I leave it so someone else to find a better solution for this bug.

comments.author: myin142

comments.author: dayanfcosta
comments.body: @adesheddie1 which version are you using?

comments.author: kewli67
comments.body:
v1.8 is working. I have the same issue. Tested with Angular 8 and Exceljs up to 3.4.
As soon as there is a route loading (or lazy load) that contains a call to Exceljs the entire application will have strange behaviour. List item with long loading,....

comments.author: samclaus
comments.body: The underlying issue is that ExcelJS (or CoreJS?) unconditionally polyfills the global `Promise` API. This breaks new Angular because Angular itself replaces the vanilla `Promise` implementation with its own `ZoneAwarePromise` that tells Angular to re-digest (render) whenever a promise fulfills or rejects. Importing ExcelJS overwrites that and thus there is no longer code to tell Angular to spin up whenever a promise settles.

comments.author: dayanfcosta
comments.body: @samterainsights so is there no way to use both together ?

comments.author: samclaus
comments.body: @dayanfcosta Not that I know of so far. They offer an `exceljs.bare.min.js` in the NPM package `/dist/` directory which doesn't include the problematic polyfill, but it is really annoying to go and modify that `package.json` every time you do an NPM install so that Webpack imports bundle that as the main file. On top of that, they use Babel to compile ExcelJS which means they require a bunch of other polyfills that you will have to include manually in your project if you use the `bare` edition. I have not put more than an hour of research into this problem so far but I will need to tackle it eventually in order to finish rewriting a company application in new Angular. I will update this thread with a solution as soon as I find one.

comments.author: Saadika
comments.body:
Hi,
Has this issue been fixed yet? I have to click twice for my pages to load while using Exceljs. None of the suggestions above worked for me.

comments.author: tsiegleauq
comments.body:
I stumbled upon the same error. Merely declaring a Workbook() inside any function was enough to cause an angular module to crash in Firefox ESR.
Downgrading to "1.15.0" fixed it for me.

comments.author: onur-ozguzel
tsiegleauq
comments.body: I cannot reproduce this error anymore on `v4.1.1`

comments.author: onur-ozguzel
comments.body:
> 
> 
> I cannot reproduce this error anymore on `v4.1.1`

Hi there;

Thanks for information.

For now i can' t use more recent version more than 3.1.0, because in 4.0.1 exceljs starts to use Async iterators, which as far as i know Angular 8 doesn' t support. :(

If it' s fixed in 4.1.1, i' ll try to look previous versions of 3.xx, because for now i cannot update Angular.

Thanks.

comments.author: onur-ozguzel
comments.body:
Hi there;

I tried all of the 3xx versions but the problem stil occurs. I cannot downgrade before 3xx because then i' m having build errors.

I tried to remove lazy loading in the page which is using exceljs. After i removed lazy loading this error started to occured in the console:

```
main.ts:12 Error: Zone.js has detected that ZoneAwarePromise `(window|global).Promise` has been overwritten.
Most likely cause is that a Promise polyfill has been loaded after Zone.js (Polyfilling Promise api is not necessary when zone.js is loaded. If you must load one, do so before loading zone.js.)
    at Function.assertZonePatched (zone-evergreen.js:52)
    at new NgZone (core.js:39457)
    at getNgZone (core.js:40708)
    at PlatformRef.bootstrapModuleFactory (core.js:40523)
    at core.js:40604
    at exceljs.min.js:3
```

Any ideas?

comments.author: onur-ozguzel
comments.body:
Ok so far i' ve changed my pollyfills.ts like this:
```

import 'exceljs'; // will be removed in Angular 8 > & excejs > 4.0.0
import 'zone.js/dist/zone';  // Included with Angular CLI.
```

After i' ve upgraded both Angular & exceljs i' ll remove this exceljs import & make the module lazy load again.

For now i hope to it' ll work like this.

Thank you all.
